frameworks.name: mid-level perceptual organization
frameworks.one_line: intermediate shape description persists across saccades to guide post-saccadic analysis

frameworks.name: Gestalt grouping
frameworks.one_line: contour grouping by similarity and collinearity tested as transferable cues

frameworks.name: trans-saccadic integration
frameworks.one_line: peripheral preview information carries forward across gaze shifts

core_question: Does perceptual grouping of object contours extracted from a peripheral preview transfer across a saccade to facilitate post-saccadic analysis of the same object?
key_assumptions: peripheral preview yields a usable mid-level shape description
grouping cues (similarity, collinearity) operate on a common intermediate representation
saccade timing isolates pre- vs post-saccadic processing
manipulations: presence vs absence of grouped contour in peripheral preview
grouping cue defining the contour (similarity vs collinearity)
match vs change of grouping cue across the saccade
key_findings: participants grouped contour elements faster when the same contour had been grouped in the preview
preview facilitation persisted despite a change in the grouping cue between preview and post-saccadic display
interpretations: an intermediate-level description of object shape is retained across gaze shifts
this trans-saccadic shape representation supports efficient and continuous scene exploration
